name: lumberjack
description: A glance tool for your git worktree fleet — colored-square status at a glance, drill into commits and files, with native diff and source.

lumberjack.repoPath.description: Absolute path to the git repo whose worktree fleet to survey. Empty = the first workspace folder.

lumberjack.commitWindow.description: How many recent commits to render as squares per row.

lumberjack.salvageBranch.description: Preserve branch that 'Salvage & Fell' appends a WIP snapshot to.

lumberjack.trunk.description: Trunk branch to color against. Empty = auto-detect (master, then main).

lumberjack.statusBarRefreshSeconds.description: How often (seconds) the status-bar tile re-reads the fleet. 0 disables the periodic refresh.

lumberjack.warmOnStartup.description: Interrogate the fleet in the background at startup so the first open is instant and current.

lumberjack.cacheFreshnessSeconds.description: How long a warmed cache is trusted as current — opening within this window paints instantly with no re-gather. The refresh button always forces a live read.

scripts.test: npm run compile && node --test test/*.test.js test/git/*.test.js
